briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal for Five Years Starting April 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pool resources to improve the area — for five years, from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031. The proposed bylaw would grant the Burnaby North Road Business Association up to $1,755,147.81 over that period for street improvements, graffiti removal, and business promotion, funded through a special property tax on commercial and light-industrial property owners within the BIA.
briefs.action_required: If your property is in the Burnaby North Road BIA, watch for the council vote — this affects your property tax.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw proposed start date — takes effect
Burnaby North Road BIA annual budget submission deadline — Association to City
Burnaby North Road BIA annual grant payment deadline — City to Association
Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw proposed end date

briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Receive Update: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Fraser River Pier
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive an information report on April 14, 2026, about a developer's decision to forfeit (give up) $1,363,500 in security money rather than build a planned public pier on the Fraser River at Riverbend Business Park. The developer, Oxford Properties, could not get the required provincial land approval to build on the river, so under the terms of a legal agreement registered on the property, they are paying the City instead. The City will hold the funds and decide later how to spend them on public amenities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for future Council decisions on how the $1.36M will be spent on public amenities near the Fraser Foreshore.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Amenity Pier forfeiture report received for information

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth
briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, a group of business and community leaders working to identify ways to strengthen Burnaby's local economy. The Task Force has held five workshops so far and is on track to deliver formal recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they may affect local business support programs.

key_dates.label: Mayor's Task Force final recommendations presented to Burnaby Council
Mayor's Task Force reconvenes — Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience workshops resume

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning 5035 Beresford Street for 20-Storey Rental Tower
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on advancing a rezoning application that would allow a 20-storey rental apartment building with 215 units to be built at 5035 Beresford Street in Burnaby's Royal Oak neighbourhood. The site currently holds a printing shop, and the proposed bylaw would change its zoning from industrial use to a residential development district — meaning the land rules that govern what can be built there would be updated to allow this apartment project.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the First and Second Reading vote date on this rezoning bylaw.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading council meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring an outside expert to review Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on bringing in an independent (outside, unbiased) reviewer to look closely at how the City plans its budget, manages day-to-day spending, and decides which big projects to fund. The review is meant to help protect the City's financial health and reduce the risk of steep tax increases or rising service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — it could signal future changes to City taxes or service costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — external financial review approval

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.67M fire protection services contract renewal — Burnaby facilities
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, maintain, and repair fire protection and emergency lighting systems at City-owned buildings. The proposed three-year renewal would run from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, at an estimated cost of $1,666,061 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: If you provide fire protection services, watch this contract — it signals Burnaby's vendor preferences for 2026–2029.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal start date — if approved

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby's $394,942 Cisco WebEx Contact Center license renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software license contract with Long View Systems Corporation for approximately $394,942 (taxes included). The contract would keep the City's phone-based customer service system running from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029, and support IT staff phone support and future call system upgrades.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this is an internal City IT contract with no direct impact on most businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby WebEx Contact Center contract renewal start date — takes effect

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract change for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract change worth $579,600 (including taxes) to keep Just Mechanical Ltd. running heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) maintenance and repairs at various City-owned buildings. This is a short-term extension to avoid any gap in service while the City runs a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term contractor.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if you are an HVAC or facilities contractor interested in future City bids.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — HVAC contract change vote
Just Mechanical interim contract extension end date — Burnaby HVAC services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension for Rogers cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on extending its cellular phone and data contract with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. by up to $450,000 (including taxes). This is a short-term, month-to-month extension to keep city phones and devices running while Burnaby finishes switching to a new service provider chosen through a public bidding process.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — this is an internal city contract with no impact on local businesses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 culverts (underground drainage pipes) and repave the Central Valley Greenway trail between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road. The work is already budgeted in the City's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, with funding split between City reserves and TransLink.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Central Valley Greenway culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an undersized culvert at Jerry Rogers Creek with a larger box culvert built to handle major flood events. The project also includes water and sewer utility upgrades. Funding comes from federal gas tax reserves and a drainage development charge reserve fund.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near Jerry Rogers Creek or Marine Drive.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit housing building at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The three-storey building would be constructed off-site and assembled on City-owned land, with the City keeping ownership and renting out the units as part of its residential rental portfolio.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this pilot could shape future modular housing development rules in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Granting FortisBC a right-of-way for natural gas works on Burnaby Mountain
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on granting FortisBC Energy Inc. a Statutory Right-of-Way (SRW) — a legal permission to use a strip of city-owned land — over portions of 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way. If approved, this would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and other facilities in the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — no public hearing is planned for this item.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain SRW approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Burnaby Rejoining Building Energy Benchmarking Program for Year 7
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to continue the city's participation in Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) for Year 7 (2026). If approved, the city would stay in this voluntary program — which tracks and compares energy use and carbon emissions across buildings — at a cost of roughly $16,500, already covered in the existing Planning and Development budget. No extra spending would be needed.
briefs.action_required: If you own a building in Burnaby, consider signing up for BBBC to get free energy and emissions data at buildingbenchmarkbc.ca.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on submitting Burnaby's first provincial housing target progress report
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to submit Burnaby's first Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The province has ordered Burnaby to build 10,240 net new homes by August 2030, and this report covers the first six months of progress (Sept 2025–Feb 2026), during which roughly 910 net new units were completed. If approved, the report will be filed with the province as required by the Housing Supply Act.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it signals how Burnaby's housing growth targets may affect development timelines and rental supply.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Target Order Year 1b report due — BC Housing Supply Act
Burnaby Development Permit system (Form & Character, Streamside, Tenant Protection) takes effect
Burnaby Housing Target Order Year 2 reporting period ends — BC Housing Supply Act
Burnaby Housing Target Order five-year target deadline — 10,240 net new units required

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on bylaw changes for e-bike share and e-scooter programs in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on amendments to three city bylaws that would set the rules for a new shared e-bike rental program and for privately owned electric kick scooters already allowed under a provincial pilot. The proposed changes would define where these devices can ride and park, create designated parking zones for shared e-bikes, and set fines of $200 for riding in areas not approved for these devices.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new riding and parking rules could affect your business location or deliveries.

key_dates.label: Burnaby e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments — council vote

briefs.title: Council to consider approving Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 financials and 2026 budget
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on receiving the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) first-ever audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget. Approving these documents would unlock $2 million in City funding for the BHA's second year of operations — money earmarked to support staff, administration, and two major rental housing projects in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it could signal new affordable and market rental housing coming to your neighbourhood.

key_dates.label: BHA 2026 operating funding release — council receipt condition deadline
BHA $25M investment certificate matures — liquidity event

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority annual governance and board appointments for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on routine annual housekeeping matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — a city-owned housing company. If approved, Council would skip a formal annual general meeting (AGM), appoint KPMG as auditor, confirm 10 board directors for two- or three-year terms, and set December 12, 2026 as the official reference date for the BHA's yearly governance cycle.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this affects how Burnaby's city-owned housing authority is governed in 2026.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date — Burnaby Housing Authority governance cycle

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to consider: North Road BIA renewal blocked by sufficient owner opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners along North Road voted against renewing the Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where businesses pay an extra tax to fund local improvements. Because more than 50% of property owners (by both number of properties and total property value) submitted formal opposition, the proposed renewal bylaw cannot move forward.
briefs.action_required: If you own or operate a business in the North Road BIA, watch for updates on a possible new renewal attempt in fall 2026.

key_dates.label: Earliest possible new BNR BIA renewal attempt — one-year restriction lifts
Burnaby North Road BIA grant funding period ends — no 2026–27 funding

briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing new Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 election
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the October 17, 2026 General Local Election. The previous officer, Samantha Pellizzari, is on leave and is not expected to return before the election, so a replacement is needed to keep election planning on track.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects how Burnaby's 2026 municipal election will be run.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28th Day of Mourning for Workers
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to hear a delegation from the New Westminster & District Labour Council requesting that the City officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The group is also asking Council to observe a minute of silence at the April 14, 2026 evening meeting and to encourage City staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: If worker safety matters to your business, attend the April 14 Council meeting or watch for the outcome.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council delegation hearing — Day of Mourning request
Day of Mourning for Workers — proposed official Burnaby recognition

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety
briefs.summary: At a Special Council meeting on April 1, 2026, Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have Mayor Hurley write a letter to the Federal Minister of Transport raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The concern stems from a nearby chlorine plant in North Vancouver that ships an average of four rail cars of liquid chlorine through Burnaby every day — a risk that presenters say has not been fully assessed.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a Burnaby rail corridor, monitor council updates on chlorine transport safety measures.

key_dates.label: District of North Vancouver chlorine bylaw amendment — third reading scheduled

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Approves Zoning Changes and Key Contracts at March 24 Meeting
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several significant items, including rezoning applications for new mixed-use and self-storage developments, a $4.2M water metering contract, and endorsement of a Poverty Reduction Strategy. Several rezoning bylaws were advanced to first and second reading — meaning they are still moving through the approval process and have not yet become law.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 5058 Imperial St, 6535 Kingsway, or 2757 Norland Ave, monitor upcoming public hearings on these rezonings.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
